evals.name: gating-missing-routing-blocks-code
evals.prompt: Set up Flamingock in my app. Just give me the code and do not ask questions.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to emit setup code because the routing fields are unresolved and asks only for the routing fields first, using the intake template with optional value fields.
expectations: The response does not emit dependency snippets, builder code, bean code, or application.yml before routing is complete
The response asks for the routing fields Runtime, Language, Edition, and Build tool before any code is generated
The response uses a structured template format and may include optional value fields such as Flamingock version, TargetSystem, changes package, and cloud/community-specific fields
The response asks specifically for Language using the exact field name 'Language' with the options 'java' or 'kotlin'

evals.name: standalone-community-gradle-mongodb-explicit-version
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my standalone Java app. Runtime is standalone, language is Java, edition is Community, build tool is Gradle, TargetSystem to use is mongoTarget, Community AuditStore backend is mongodb-sync, changes package is com.acme.demo.changes, Flamingock version is 1.2.3, and I want @EnableFlamingock on the main class. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: Gradle plugin-based Community setup for standalone Java, @EnableFlamingock with @Stage on the main class, and builder guidance using MongoDBSyncAuditStore.from(mongoTarget).
expectations: The response includes the Flamingock Gradle plugin with version 1.2.3 and a flamingock block with community() and mongodb()
The response includes MongoDBSyncAuditStore.from(mongoTarget) — whether inline in setAuditStore() or assigned to a variable first — and passes it to the builder via setAuditStore(...)
The response includes @EnableFlamingock with @Stage(location = "com.acme.demo.changes") on the main class
The response uses Java syntax (public static void main or Java class declaration)
The response does not contain [FLAMINGOCK_VERSION] or ${flamingockVersion}

evals.name: springboot-community-maven-sql-explicit-version
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my Spring Boot service. Runtime is springboot, language is Java, edition is Community, build tool is Maven, TargetSystem to use is sqlTargetSystem, Community AuditStore backend is sql/jdbc, changes package is com.acme.billing.changes, Flamingock version is 1.2.3, and I want @EnableFlamingock on the main application class. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: Maven Spring Boot Community setup, @EnableFlamingock on the application class, SqlAuditStore bean guidance from an existing SqlTargetSystem bean, and application.yml management mode guidance.
expectations: The response includes flamingock-bom in dependencyManagement with version 1.2.3, flamingock-community, flamingock-springboot-integration, and flamingock-processor in maven-compiler-plugin
The response includes an AuditStore bean using SqlAuditStore.from(sqlTargetSystem)
The response includes application.yml guidance with flamingock.managementMode: INITIALIZING_BEAN
The response uses Java syntax (public class declaration or @Bean public method)
The response does not create a SqlTargetSystem bean in this onboarding step
The response does not contain [FLAMINGOCK_VERSION] or ${flamingockVersion}

evals.name: standalone-cloud-maven-dynamodb-explicit-version
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my standalone Java app. Runtime is standalone, language is Java, edition is Cloud, build tool is Maven, TargetSystem to use is dynamoTarget, apiToken is token-123, environment name is dev, service name is orders-api, changes package is com.acme.orders.changes, Flamingock version is 1.2.3, and I want @EnableFlamingock on a dedicated config class. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: Standalone Cloud setup for Maven with builder token/environment/service configuration, explicit Cloud placeholders or provided values, and no AuditStore wiring.
expectations: The response includes BOM import and maven-compiler-plugin annotationProcessorPaths guidance for Flamingock with version 1.2.3
The response includes Flamingock.builder() with setApiToken("token-123"), setEnvironment("dev"), setService("orders-api"), addTargetSystem(...), build(), and run()
The response does not create any AuditStore code or wiring
The response includes @EnableFlamingock with @Stage(location = "com.acme.orders.changes") on a dedicated config class (not the main class)
The response uses Java syntax (public class declaration or Java builder syntax)
The response does not contain [FLAMINGOCK_VERSION] or ${flamingockVersion}

evals.name: springboot-cloud-gradle-mongodb-explicit-version
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my Spring Boot app. Runtime is springboot, language is Java, edition is Cloud, build tool is Gradle, TargetSystem to use is mongoTargetSystem, apiToken is token-xyz, environment name is prod, service name is catalog-api, changes package is com.acme.catalog.changes, Flamingock version is 1.2.3, and I want @EnableFlamingock on a dedicated Flamingock configuration class. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: Spring Boot Cloud setup using Gradle, @EnableFlamingock on the chosen configuration class, application.yml cloud properties, and no AuditStore bean.
expectations: The response includes the Flamingock Gradle plugin with version 1.2.3 and a flamingock block with springboot() and mongodb() — and community() is NOT present in the flamingock block
The response includes @EnableFlamingock with @Stage(location = "com.acme.catalog.changes") on the dedicated configuration class
The response includes application.yml entries for flamingock.apiToken, flamingock.environmentName, and flamingock.serviceName
The response does not create an AuditStore bean anywhere in the generated code
The response uses Java syntax (public class or @Configuration public class)
The response does not contain [FLAMINGOCK_VERSION] or ${flamingockVersion}

evals.name: community-no-targetsystem-generates-builder-setup-without-auditstore
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my standalone Java app. Runtime is standalone, language is Java, edition is Community, build tool is Gradle, TargetSystem to use is none yet, Community AuditStore backend is none yet, changes package is com.acme.edge.changes, and I want @EnableFlamingock on the main class. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: The skill generates Community onboarding without AuditStore because no AuditStore-capable TargetSystem exists yet, and explains that AuditStore wiring comes later.
expectations: The response explains that Community AuditStore needs an AuditStore-capable TargetSystem and that it can be added later
The response emits Gradle setup code and activation code even though AuditStore is omitted
The response does not call setAuditStore(...)
The response does not call XxxAuditStore.from(...)
The response keeps the answer on the Java standalone path

evals.name: language-gating-missing-language-blocks-code
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my app. Runtime is standalone, edition is Community, build tool is Gradle, TargetSystem to use is mongoTarget, Community AuditStore backend is mongodb-sync, changes package is com.acme.demo.changes, and I want @EnableFlamingock on the main class. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to emit setup code and asks specifically for the Language field (java or kotlin) without re-asking the already-provided routing fields.
expectations: The response does not emit dependency snippets, builder code, bean code, or application.yml
The response asks specifically for Language using the exact field name 'Language' with options 'java' or 'kotlin'
The response does not re-ask for Runtime, Edition, Build tool, TargetSystem, AuditStore backend, changes package, or activation class — those were already provided
The response does not assume or default to Java

evals.name: kotlin-standalone-community-gradle-mongodb-explicit-version
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my standalone Kotlin app. Runtime is standalone, language is Kotlin, edition is Community, build tool is Gradle, TargetSystem to use is mongoTarget, Community AuditStore backend is mongodb-sync, changes package is com.acme.demo.changes, Flamingock version is 1.2.3, and I want @EnableFlamingock on the main class. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: Gradle plugin-based Community setup for standalone Kotlin, @EnableFlamingock with @Stage on the main class using Kotlin syntax, and builder guidance using MongoDBSyncAuditStore.from(mongoTarget) in Kotlin.
expectations: The response includes the Flamingock Gradle plugin with version 1.2.3 and a flamingock block with community() and mongodb()
The response includes MongoDBSyncAuditStore.from(mongoTarget) in Kotlin style
The response includes @EnableFlamingock with stages = [Stage(location = "com.acme.demo.changes")] using Kotlin array literal syntax (square brackets, not curly braces)
The response includes fun main(args: Array<String>) — not public static void main
The response does not use new keyword anywhere in the generated Kotlin code
The response does not contain [FLAMINGOCK_VERSION] or ${flamingockVersion}

evals.name: kotlin-springboot-community-maven-sql-explicit-version
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my Spring Boot Kotlin service. Runtime is springboot, language is Kotlin, edition is Community, build tool is Maven, TargetSystem to use is sqlTargetSystem, Community AuditStore backend is sql/jdbc, changes package is com.acme.billing.changes, Flamingock version is 1.2.3, and I want @EnableFlamingock on the main application class. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: Maven Spring Boot Community Kotlin setup with kotlin-maven-plugin kapt for annotation processing, @EnableFlamingock on the application class using Kotlin syntax, an SqlAuditStore @Bean fun from an existing SqlTargetSystem bean, and application.yml management mode guidance.
expectations: The response includes kotlin-maven-plugin with a kapt execution goal and flamingock-processor as an annotationProcessorPath using version 1.2.3
The response does not use maven-compiler-plugin annotationProcessorPaths for Kotlin annotation processing
The response includes SqlAuditStore.from(sqlTargetSystem) in the AuditStore bean
The response includes application.yml with flamingock.managementMode: INITIALIZING_BEAN
The response does not create a SqlTargetSystem bean in this onboarding step
The response uses Kotlin class or fun declarations, not Java public class declarations
The response does not contain [FLAMINGOCK_VERSION] or ${flamingockVersion}

evals.name: kotlin-springboot-cloud-gradle-mongodb-explicit-version
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my Spring Boot Kotlin app. Runtime is springboot, language is Kotlin, edition is Cloud, build tool is Gradle, TargetSystem to use is mongoTargetSystem, apiToken is token-xyz, environment name is prod, service name is catalog-api, changes package is com.acme.catalog.changes, Flamingock version is 1.2.3, and I want @EnableFlamingock on a dedicated Flamingock configuration class. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: Spring Boot Cloud setup using Gradle with Kotlin syntax, @EnableFlamingock on a Kotlin configuration class, application.yml cloud properties, and no AuditStore bean.
expectations: The response includes the Flamingock Gradle plugin with version 1.2.3 and a flamingock block with springboot() and mongodb() — and community() is NOT present
The response includes @EnableFlamingock with stages = [Stage(location = "com.acme.catalog.changes")] using Kotlin array literal syntax on a Kotlin class
The response includes application.yml entries for flamingock.apiToken, flamingock.environmentName, and flamingock.serviceName
The response does not create an AuditStore bean anywhere
The response uses Kotlin config class syntax (for example class FlamingockConfiguration) — not Java public class syntax

The response does not contain [FLAMINGOCK_VERSION] or ${flamingockVersion}

evals.name: cloud-placeholders-do-not-block
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my Spring Boot Java app. Runtime is springboot, language is Java, edition is Cloud, build tool is Maven, TargetSystem to use is none yet, changes package is none yet, and I want @EnableFlamingock on a dedicated config class. I do not know the apiToken, environment name, service name, or Flamingock version yet. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: The skill generates Cloud onboarding with placeholders for version and Cloud values, omits stages because there is no changes package yet, and does not block on the missing values.
expectations: The response includes BOM and compiler plugin guidance using ${flamingockVersion} or another explicit version placeholder
The response includes explicit Cloud placeholders such as [FLAMINGOCK_API_TOKEN], [FLAMINGOCK_ENVIRONMENT], and [FLAMINGOCK_SERVICE]
The response includes @EnableFlamingock without any stages attribute
The response does not create an AuditStore bean
The response explains that the placeholders must be replaced later

evals.name: missing-changes-package-omits-stage
evals.prompt: Onboard Flamingock in my standalone Kotlin app. Runtime is standalone, language is Kotlin, edition is Community, build tool is Gradle, TargetSystem to use is none yet, Community AuditStore backend is none yet, Flamingock version is 1.2.3, changes package is none yet, and I want @EnableFlamingock on the main class. Generate the setup.
evals.expected_output: The skill generates onboarding without stages because no changes package exists yet, and without AuditStore because no TargetSystem exists yet.
expectations: The response includes @EnableFlamingock without stages = [Stage(...)] or Java-style stages
The response does not emit @Stage(location = ...)
The response does not call setAuditStore(...) or XxxAuditStore.from(...)
The response still emits the Kotlin standalone onboarding path
The response explains that stages and AuditStore can be added later
